fig01:
%
  Accuracy of four methods across five benchmarks; OursModel (*) wins on every task.
  \textbf{OursModel improves over the strongest baseline (Method-B) by 3.3--5.3 pp across benchmarks, with the largest gain on HumanEval (+5.3 pp) and GPQA (+5.2 pp).}
  Bars show mean accuracy (\%); error bars span $\pm$1 standard deviation.
  Hatching distinguishes methods for greyscale readers;
  (*) denotes the overall winner (OursModel).
  $n=5$ seeds per condition; std is the sample standard deviation.%
fig02:
%
  Validation loss over 50 training steps for three methods.
  \textbf{OursModel reaches a final-step loss of $0.839$, a gap of $\Delta=0.155$ below Method-A ($0.994$) and $0.350$ below Baseline ($1.189$).}
  Shaded bands show 95\% confidence intervals (mean $\pm$ CI half-width); the $\Delta$ annotation marks the gap between OursModel and Method-A at step~49.
  $n=5$ seeds; error bands are $\pm$\,CI$_{95}$ computed per step across seeds.
fig03:
Chinchilla-style scaling law: validation loss decreases with model size across 80 models.
Each point is a trained model; the dashed line is the OLS fit ($y = -0.045x + 0.621$).
\textbf{$R^2 = 0.877$}, indicating that $\log_{10}(\text{parameters})$ explains 88\% of variance in $\log_{10}(\text{validation loss})$.
The star (\textcolor{orange}{$\bigstar$}) marks OursModel at $(\log_{10}\!\text{params}=9.449,\ \text{loss}=0.210)$, nearest the target operating point.
$n=80$ models; OLS fit with no regularisation; error bars not shown (single-run evaluations).
fig04:
%
  \textbf{Four-panel overview of the proposed method.}
  %
  (\textbf{A})~Method overview: a three-stage pipeline comprising
  Input~Encoding (self-attention and positional embedding),
  Attentive~Memory (episodic memory cache), and
  Adaptive~Decoding (residual skill adapter), connected by
  feed-forward passes from Input to Output.
  %
  (\textbf{B})~Ablation study on five variants;
  removing the attention module (\texttt{-attn}) causes the largest
  drop (\textbf{$-$6.5 pp} from 64.7\% to 58.2\%), confirming
  attention as the most critical component.
  Bars show per-variant accuracy (\%); full model highlighted in blue.
  %
  (\textbf{C})~Training loss curves over 30k steps (log-scale);
  Ours (blue) converges faster and reaches a \textbf{final loss
  \(\mathbf{0.36}\) vs.\ 0.19 lower than Baseline~(0.36)},
  annotated with a double-headed arrow at the terminal step.
  %
  (\textbf{D})~5\(\times\)5 sensitivity sweep over five
  sensitivity parameters (S0--S4) and five probe depths (P0--P4);
  cell values are scores in $[0,1]$; high-scoring cells ($>$0.85)
  are bold-annotated.
  %
  Error bars and confidence intervals are not shown (single-run data
  from \texttt{data.json}); statistical uncertainty should be
  reported in future replicated experiments.
